---
type: fact
situation: When building a command-line application in Go
subject: List command output
predicate: supports --json for machine use and an aligned table for humans, with color auto-disabled
object: list --json emits raw []T JSON (no pretty-print). Default output is an aligned table via text/tabwriter or manual padding computed from plain-text widths (never from ANSI-escaped widths — colors must not corrupt column alignment). Color is ANSI escape codes for priority levels (red/yellow/green) and overdue items (bold red); ColorEnabled() returns false when os.Getenv("NO_COLOR") != "" or stdout is not a TTY (os.ModeCharDevice check). list --tag <t> filters by tag; search <q> does case-insensitive substring match across title and tags. list --archived reads the archive store.
luhmann: "6"
created: "2026-05-30"
source: distilled L2 from build episodes
---

Information learned: when in building a command-line application in Go, List command output supports --json for machine use and an aligned table for humans, with color auto-disabled list --json emits raw []T JSON (no pretty-print). Default output is an aligned table via text/tabwriter or manual padding computed from plain-text widths (never from ANSI-escaped widths — colors must not corrupt column alignment). Color is ANSI escape codes for priority levels (red/yellow/green) and overdue items (bold red); ColorEnabled() returns false when os.Getenv("NO_COLOR") != "" or stdout is not a TTY (os.ModeCharDevice check). list --tag <t> filters by tag; search <q> does case-insensitive substring match across title and tags. list --archived reads the archive store..
